tickets.subject: corelight-fleetd failing to restart
tickets.description:
Good morning, 
We deleted "corelight-fleetd.pem" and after restarting the service: "systemctl restart corelight-fleetd", Fleet manager won't restart. Can we please get assistance on this?

events.subject: [Zendesk] #{{ticket.id}} {{ current_user.name }} [{{ticket.organization.name}}]: {{ticket.title}}
[Zendesk] #{{ticket.id}} {{ current_user.name }} [{{ticket.organization.name}}]: {{ticket.title}}

events.body:
You are registered as a CC on this support request ({{ticket.id}}). Reply to this email to add a comment to the request.

{{ticket.comments_formatted}}

Fleet Manager will not start without the product identity certificate file (`corelight-fleetd.pem`) in the `/etc` directory. This file is required for licensing and sensor communications. You must restore the correct `corelight-fleetd.pem` file to `/etc`, set permissions (`sudo chmod 400 /etc/corelight-fleetd.pem`), set ownership (`sudo chown corelight-fleetd:corelight-fleetd /etc/corelight-fleetd.pem`), and then restart the service (`sudo systemctl restart corelight-fleetd`) — see official documentation and certificate copy instructions.

If you do not have a backup of the certificate, you must contact your Corelight account team or sales representative to obtain a replacement, as the service cannot function without it.

Sources:
- How to copy Fleet product identity certificate to a new host
events.html_body:

If you do not have a backup of the certificate, you must contact your Corelight account team or sales representative to obtain a replacement, as the service cannot function without it.

events.subject: [Zendesk] #{{ticket.id}} {{ current_user.name }} [{{ticket.organization.name}}]: {{ticket.title}}

events.body:
Lyne,

Fleet Manager will not start without the product identity certificate file (`corelight-fleetd.pem`) in the `/etc` directory. This file is required for licensing and sensor communications.  To restore the Fleet functionality, you must restore a current Fleet license file (corelight-fleetd.pem) into this directory with the correct file permissions.

If you do not have a backup of the certificate or if your previous certificate was expired, please let me know and I can request an updated file from the licensing team.  If you need any assistance recovering this file, please let me know.

Thank you,

Michael Hawks
Senior Escalation Engineer
Corelight

For customer support, please email support@corelight.com, call directly at +1 (336) 933-9335 or toll-free at +1(888) 547-9497, and select option 2.
events.html_body:
Lyne,
 
Fleet Manager will not start without the product identity certificate file (corelight-fleetd.pem) in the /etc directory. This file is required for licensing and sensor communications.  To restore the Fleet functionality, you must restore a current Fleet license file (corelight-fleetd.pem) into this directory with the correct file permissions.   
 
If you do not have a backup of the certificate or if your previous certificate was expired, please let me know and I can request an updated file from the licensing team.  If you need any assistance recovering this file, please let me know.
 
Thank you,

Michael Hawks
Senior Escalation Engineer
Corelight

For customer support, please email support@corelight.com, call directly at +1 (336) 933-9335 or toll-free at +1(888) 547-9497, and select option 2.

events.subject: [Zendesk] #{{ticket.id}} {{ current_user.name }} [{{ticket.organization.name}}]: {{ticket.title}}

events.body:
You are registered as a CC on this support request ({{ticket.id}}). Reply to this email to add a comment to the request.

{{ticket.comments_formatted}}

Lyne,

You can use a public CA-signed or Enterprise CA-signed certificate for the Fleet Manager WebUI by configuring the `ssl-certificate-path` and `ssl-key-path` fields in `/etc/corelight-fleetd.conf` to point to your custom certificate and key.  However, the corelight-fleetd.pem identity certificate (provided by Corelight) must still be used for sensor-to-Fleet Manager communication and licensing; this cannot be replaced with a public/private CA certificate (`certificate-path` / `key-path` fields in `/etc/corelight-fleetd.conf` ).

Reference:
https://docs.corelight.cloud/fleet/installation/fleet-host-install.html#use-a-tls-certificate-with-fm

Our license records do show that your current Fleet Manager license does expire on 6/9/2026, which coincides with the error messages in your screenshot.   If a license renewal is currently being processed, you will receive the new Fleet Manager license certificate once the renewal process has completed.

We can set up a call to discuss further.  Please provide a few dates and times when you are available and I will arrange a meeting.

Thank you,

Michael Hawks
Senior Escalation Engineer
Corelight

For customer support, please email support@corelight.com, call directly at +1 (336) 933-9335 or toll-free at +1(888) 547-9497, and select option 2.
events.html_body:
Lyne,
 
You can use a public CA-signed or Enterprise CA-signed certificate for the Fleet Manager WebUI by configuring the ssl-certificate-path and ssl-key-path fields in /etc/corelight-fleetd.conf to point to your custom certificate and key.  However, the corelight-fleetd.pem identity certificate (provided by Corelight) must still be used for sensor-to-Fleet Manager communication and licensing; this cannot be replaced with a public/private CA certificate (certificate-path / key-path fields in /etc/corelight-fleetd.conf ). 
 
Reference:
https://docs.corelight.cloud/fleet/installation/fleet-host-install.html#use-a-tls-certificate-with-fm
 
Our license records do show that your current Fleet Manager license does expire on 6/9/2026, which coincides with the error messages in your screenshot.   If a license renewal is currently being processed, you will receive the new Fleet Manager license certificate once the renewal process has completed.      
 
We can set up a call to discuss further.  Please provide a few dates and times when you are available and I will arrange a meeting.
 
Thank you,

Michael Hawks
Senior Escalation Engineer
Corelight

For customer support, please email support@corelight.com, call directly at +1 (336) 933-9335 or toll-free at +1(888) 547-9497, and select option 2.

events.body:
First, check the service status and logs on the affected sensors. Confirm that the monitoring ports are receiving traffic and that the configuration options for log export are correct. Also, review the service status for any failures and ensure the server-side configuration matches the sensor settings. See Common Issues Troubleshooting.

If logs are still not generating, review the release notes and known issues for v28.4.x. There have been fixes for exporter failures and log generation issues in recent versions, but no specific issue for logs not generating in v28.4.6 is documented. If the problem persists after checking configuration and service status, contact Corelight Support for further assistance. See Sensor Known Issues and How to open a support case.

Would you like a step-by-step troubleshooting guide?

Sources:
- Sensor Known Issues
- How to open a support case

events.body:
For context: we received the following error:
Failed to GET configuration from sensor, err: status=500 Internal Server Error, body:"{\n    \"title\": \"Unhandled exception\",\n    \"description\": \"See broala-log.log on Corelight Sensor for details.\"\n}"

On most of the sensors so far, it has cleared after about 40-50 minutes through self-repair.  On 22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e, it has been much longer than that and still not cleared.  Log flow is interrupted during this period for any of the devices.

Hey Travis,

It appears that right as we opened the case, some of them started to go into the Kubernetes self-repair sequence we’ve seen before, which fixed the other devices. We’re waiting for them to show back up in Fleet Manager now (which is probably also delaying the remote interface being enabled).

As Taylor mentioned, we turned on the remote interface for those UID’s as they have been stuck trying to download updates. They’re at two of our remote locations with less reliable internet.

Regards,

events.body:
Hi Jacob and Taylor,

Thank you for the additional information.

Confirming that I am seeing the same status as you are, and `36-ad-d2-2b-d0-26-3c-b5` shows no movement from the current "Downloading part 7/119" stage. Could you please attempt to reboot this sensor?

After the reboot, we recommend following the offline update path for this sensor. Confirming that you know the process on this? If not, I can send it through.

Thanks,
Travis Anderson

After the reboot, we recommend following the offline update path for this sensor. Confirming that you know the process on this? If not, I can send it through.

events.subject: [Zendesk] #{{ticket.id}} {{ current_user.name }} [{{ticket.organization.name}}]: {{ticket.title}}

events.body:
Hi Jacob,

Thank you for the update, and I apologise for the inconvenience you've been experiencing with this.

Could you please perform the following offline update procedure for both of these sensors:
- `f1-20-a7-22-c8-c0-b8-f2`
- `36-ad-d2-2b-d0-26-3c-b5`


Firstly, please download the 29.4.5 release image: https://support.corelight.com/hc/en-us/articles/360052242794-How-to-download-and-upgrade-offline-Sensor-versions

1. After validating the SHA-256 checksum, transfer the update image file to the offline_updater folder on the sensor. Example `scp <filename>.dat diag-shell@<sensor_ip>.:~/offline_updater`
2. SSH into the sensor.
3. Run the following command first to temporarily disconnect it from Fleet Manager: `corelight-client configuration update --fleet.enable="False"`
4. Mount the Image: `corelight-updater mount /home/diag-shell/offline_updater/<filename>.dat`
5. Apply the Update: `corelight-client updates apply`
6. Clean Up: Once the update is fully completed, unmount and remove the image file from the sensor: `corelight-updater unmount` and `rm /home/diag-shell/offline_updater/<filename>.dat`
7. Reconnect to Fleet Manager: Finally, re-add the sensor to Fleet using: `corelight-client configuration update --fleet.enable="True"`


Please let me know if the above steps are clear. I'd be more than happy to jump on a session to walk through this together if needed.

Thanks,
Travis Anderson
events.html_body:
Hi Jacob,
 
Thank you for the update, and I apologise for the inconvenience you've been experiencing with this.
 
Could you please perform the following offline update procedure for both of these sensors:
  • f1-20-a7-22-c8-c0-b8-f2
  • 36-ad-d2-2b-d0-26-3c-b5

Firstly, please download the 29.4.5 release image: https://support.corelight.com/hc/en-us/articles/360052242794-How-to-download-and-upgrade-offline-Sensor-versions
  1. After validating the SHA-256 checksum, transfer the update image file to the offline_updater folder on the sensor. Example scp <filename>.dat diag-shell@<sensor_ip>.:~/offline_updater
  2. SSH into the sensor.
  3. Run the following command first to temporarily disconnect it from Fleet Manager: corelight-client configuration update --fleet.enable="False"
  4. Mount the Image: corelight-updater mount /home/diag-shell/offline_updater/<filename>.dat
  5. Apply the Update: corelight-client updates apply
  6. Clean Up: Once the update is fully completed, unmount and remove the image file from the sensor: corelight-updater unmount and rm /home/diag-shell/offline_updater/<filename>.dat
  7. Reconnect to Fleet Manager: Finally, re-add the sensor to Fleet using: corelight-client configuration update --fleet.enable="True"
 
Please let me know if the above steps are clear. I'd be more than happy to jump on a session to walk through this together if needed.
 
Thanks, 
Travis Anderson

Hi Taylor,

Thank you for providing the information in the previous messages.

Could you please enable the remote interface for the sensors so that we are able to investigate further:
- `22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e`
- `36-ad-d2-2b-d0-26-3c-b5`
- `f1-20-a7-22-c8-c0-b8-f2`


For the sensors disconnected from Fleet Manager please run the following to enable the remote:

- `corelight-client configuration update --remote.management=1`


Could you please also let us know what actions below you permit us to do:

1. Observe Only
2. Restart Services
3. Config Changes
4. Test Temporary Fixes
5. Apply Permanent Fixes


Thanks,
Travis Anderson

  • f1-20-a7-22-c8-c0-b8-f2
 
For the sensors disconnected from Fleet Manager please run the following to enable the remote:
  • corelight-client configuration update --remote.management=1
 
Could you please also let us know what actions below you permit us to do:

  4. Test Temporary Fixes
  5. Apply Permanent Fixes
 
Thanks, 
Travis Anderson

events.subject: [Zendesk] #{{ticket.id}} {{ current_user.name }} [{{ticket.organization.name}}]: {{ticket.title}}

events.body:
Hello Taylor, Jacob,

Quick update, main image has downloaded, there are some maint level tarballs downloading as part of the download.

Please be assured, we are monitoring and will apply as soon as possible.

Just some background, on the deploy there are a set of images that are copied over to the container environment at the deploy time and these are the basis of the NGS environment. For some reason, only 13 were transferred (there are 60+ when extracted), and again 13 after the purge / deploy, and again the second time I cleaned up, this would lead me to want to clean up a little further and start from a good baseline with checksums at each stage of the download.

The sensor includes commands to be able to download the base images again.

https://docs.corelight.cloud/softwaresensor/corelightctl-usage/sensor/corelightctl-usage-sensor-images.html

And this is what is currently in progress, the first image is 3.6GB that takes the longest (700 KB/s), then there are some subsequent images (ml level, rke environment, cni etc) which are currently downloading, these are not quite so large, but they are downloaded in series, so I need to wait until they have all downloaded.

I just wanted to keep you up to date with progress, although it's not as far as I would have liked to be this far into the day we are constrained by download speeds currently.

Any concerns please reach out, and if you want to take a different route again please just ask.


Kind regards,
Mark Taylor
Corelight Escalation Engineering
events.html_body:
Hello Taylor, Jacob,
 
Quick update, main image has downloaded, there are some maint level tarballs downloading as part of the download.
 
Please be assured, we are monitoring and will apply as soon as possible.
 
Just some background, on the deploy there are a set of images that are copied over to the container environment at the deploy time and these are the basis of the NGS environment. For some reason, only 13 were transferred (there are 60+ when extracted), and again 13 after the purge / deploy, and again the second time I cleaned up, this would lead me to want to clean up a little further and start from a good baseline with checksums at each stage of the download.
 
The sensor includes commands to be able to download the base images again.
 
https://docs.corelight.cloud/softwaresensor/corelightctl-usage/sensor/corelightctl-usage-sensor-images.html
 
And this is what is currently in progress, the first image is 3.6GB that takes the longest (700 KB/s), then there are some subsequent images (ml level, rke environment, cni etc) which are currently downloading, these are not quite so large, but they are downloaded in series, so I need to wait until they have all downloaded.
 
I just wanted to keep you up to date with progress, although it's not as far as I would have liked to be this far into the day we are constrained by download speeds currently.
 
Any concerns please reach out, and if you want to take a different route again please just ask.
 
 
Kind regards,
Mark Taylor
Corelight Escalation Engineering

events.subject: [Zendesk] #{{ticket.id}} {{ current_user.name }} [{{ticket.organization.name}}]: {{ticket.title}}

events.body:
Summary, customer upgraded several sensors to 28.4.6, most came back online but 22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e was reporting driver issues fro napatech drivers.


    │ 2026/05/15 07:29:16 Problem connecting to ntapi: Mismatch of driver and NTAPI libraries


Query on the m/c for the drivers showed a later level in the sensor upgrade version


    [BroLin-28.4.6@gmfinancial 22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e ~]$ /opt/diag-shell/usr/bin/corelight-config nic display-firmwareDisplaying active NIC firmware …FPGA BANK0 active 9500 28.07 4x1G dedup: True shunt: False fec: FalseFPGA BANK1 9500 28.07 4x1G dedup: True shunt: False fec: FalseAvailable images:9500 28.11 4x1G dedup: True shunt: False fec: False <<<<<<


New version burned to bank1 and toggled, sensor power cycled to come up clean, the sensor was then purged, deployed (broala-apply-config), and checking the pods it looked like it had hung on the coredns, the pods were stuck in initalising, "crictl images" showed 13 images, where we sbhojld be norht of 60.

The deploy was left for a sustained period to see if the issue resolved itself, and after 45-60 minutes, sensor was purged again and deployed to see if it would get any further while checking "crictl images".  We got to 13 images, and the no further

[BroLin-28.4.6@gmfinancial 22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e log]$ sudo crictl images | wc -l
13

At this point it was suspected that there may have been an issue with the update images that had been copied/downalded to the sensor as part of the upgrade, possibly a network quality issue with the link, the customer stated the links are due to be upgraded at some point in the near future.

With the sensor stopped and purged, fresh images were downloaded using "sudo corelightctl sensor images download ; sudo corelightctl sensor images load -v", the sensor was then deployed using broala apply config and this time all of the images were populated (77'ish) and the sensor came up clean.

Post upgrade: "sudo corelightctl sensor download" puts the images into /opt/corelight, this affects the filesystem usage in the root fiilesystem, and it doesn't look like there is a way to housekeep these images as they may be required for the running of the sensor. This may be a bug where they should go into /data possibly, will need to investigte further.
events.html_body:
Summary, customer upgraded several sensors to 28.4.6, most came back online but 22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e was reporting driver issues fro napatech drivers.
 
│ 2026/05/15 07:29:16 Problem connecting to ntapi: Mismatch of driver and NTAPI libraries
 
Query on the m/c for the drivers showed a later level in the sensor upgrade version
 
[BroLin-28.4.6@gmfinancial 22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e ~]$ /opt/diag-shell/usr/bin/corelight-config nic display-firmware
Displaying active NIC firmware …
FPGA BANK0 active 9500 28.07 4x1G dedup: True shunt: False fec: False
FPGA BANK1             9500 28.07 4x1G dedup: True shunt: False fec: False

Available images:
9500 28.11 4x1G dedup: True shunt: False fec: False <<<<<<
 
New version burned to bank1 and toggled, sensor power cycled to come up clean, the sensor was then purged, deployed (broala-apply-config), and checking the pods it looked like it had hung on the coredns, the pods were stuck in initalising, "crictl images" showed 13 images, where we sbhojld be norht of 60. 
 
The deploy was left for a sustained period to see if the issue resolved itself, and after 45-60 minutes, sensor was purged again and deployed to see if it would get any further while checking "crictl images".  We got to 13 images, and the no further
 
[BroLin-28.4.6@gmfinancial 22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e log]$ sudo crictl images | wc -l
13
 
At this point it was suspected that there may have been an issue with the update images that had been copied/downalded to the sensor as part of the upgrade, possibly a network quality issue with the link, the customer stated the links are due to be upgraded at some point in the near future.
 
With the sensor stopped and purged, fresh images were downloaded using "sudo corelightctl sensor images download ; sudo corelightctl sensor images load -v", the sensor was then deployed using broala apply config and this time all of the images were populated (77'ish) and the sensor came up clean.
 
Post upgrade: "sudo corelightctl sensor download" puts the images into /opt/corelight, this affects the filesystem usage in the root fiilesystem, and it doesn't look like there is a way to housekeep these images as they may be required for the running of the sensor. This may be a bug where they should go into /data possibly, will need to investigte further.

comments.body:
First, check the service status and logs on the affected sensors. Confirm that the monitoring ports are receiving traffic and that the configuration options for log export are correct. Also, review the service status for any failures and ensure the server-side configuration matches the sensor settings. See Common Issues Troubleshooting.

If logs are still not generating, review the release notes and known issues for v28.4.x. There have been fixes for exporter failures and log generation issues in recent versions, but no specific issue for logs not generating in v28.4.6 is documented. If the problem persists after checking configuration and service status, contact Corelight Support for further assistance. See Sensor Known Issues and How to open a support case.

Would you like a step-by-step troubleshooting guide?

Sources:
- Sensor Known Issues
- How to open a support case

For context: we received the following error:
Failed to GET configuration from sensor, err: status=500 Internal Server Error, body:"{\n    \"title\": \"Unhandled exception\",\n    \"description\": \"See broala-log.log on Corelight Sensor for details.\"\n}"

On most of the sensors so far, it has cleared after about 40-50 minutes through self-repair.  On 22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e, it has been much longer than that and still not cleared.  Log flow is interrupted during this period for any of the devices.

comments.body:
Hi Jacob,

Thank you for the update, and I apologise for the inconvenience you've been experiencing with this.

Could you please perform the following offline update procedure for both of these sensors:
- `f1-20-a7-22-c8-c0-b8-f2`
- `36-ad-d2-2b-d0-26-3c-b5`


Firstly, please download the 29.4.5 release image: https://support.corelight.com/hc/en-us/articles/360052242794-How-to-download-and-upgrade-offline-Sensor-versions

1. After validating the SHA-256 checksum, transfer the update image file to the offline_updater folder on the sensor. Example `scp <filename>.dat diag-shell@<sensor_ip>.:~/offline_updater`
2. SSH into the sensor.
3. Run the following command first to temporarily disconnect it from Fleet Manager: `corelight-client configuration update --fleet.enable="False"`
4. Mount the Image: `corelight-updater mount /home/diag-shell/offline_updater/<filename>.dat`
5. Apply the Update: `corelight-client updates apply`
6. Clean Up: Once the update is fully completed, unmount and remove the image file from the sensor: `corelight-updater unmount` and `rm /home/diag-shell/offline_updater/<filename>.dat`
7. Reconnect to Fleet Manager: Finally, re-add the sensor to Fleet using: `corelight-client configuration update --fleet.enable="True"`


Please let me know if the above steps are clear. I'd be more than happy to jump on a session to walk through this together if needed.

Thanks,
Travis Anderson

comments.body:
Hello Taylor, Jacob,

Quick update, main image has downloaded, there are some maint level tarballs downloading as part of the download.

Please be assured, we are monitoring and will apply as soon as possible.

Just some background, on the deploy there are a set of images that are copied over to the container environment at the deploy time and these are the basis of the NGS environment. For some reason, only 13 were transferred (there are 60+ when extracted), and again 13 after the purge / deploy, and again the second time I cleaned up, this would lead me to want to clean up a little further and start from a good baseline with checksums at each stage of the download.

The sensor includes commands to be able to download the base images again.

https://docs.corelight.cloud/softwaresensor/corelightctl-usage/sensor/corelightctl-usage-sensor-images.html

And this is what is currently in progress, the first image is 3.6GB that takes the longest (700 KB/s), then there are some subsequent images (ml level, rke environment, cni etc) which are currently downloading, these are not quite so large, but they are downloaded in series, so I need to wait until they have all downloaded.

I just wanted to keep you up to date with progress, although it's not as far as I would have liked to be this far into the day we are constrained by download speeds currently.

Any concerns please reach out, and if you want to take a different route again please just ask.


Kind regards,
Mark Taylor
Corelight Escalation Engineering

comments.body:
Summary, customer upgraded several sensors to 28.4.6, most came back online but 22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e was reporting driver issues fro napatech drivers.


    │ 2026/05/15 07:29:16 Problem connecting to ntapi: Mismatch of driver and NTAPI libraries


Query on the m/c for the drivers showed a later level in the sensor upgrade version


    [BroLin-28.4.6@gmfinancial 22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e ~]$ /opt/diag-shell/usr/bin/corelight-config nic display-firmwareDisplaying active NIC firmware …FPGA BANK0 active 9500 28.07 4x1G dedup: True shunt: False fec: FalseFPGA BANK1 9500 28.07 4x1G dedup: True shunt: False fec: FalseAvailable images:9500 28.11 4x1G dedup: True shunt: False fec: False <<<<<<


New version burned to bank1 and toggled, sensor power cycled to come up clean, the sensor was then purged, deployed (broala-apply-config), and checking the pods it looked like it had hung on the coredns, the pods were stuck in initalising, "crictl images" showed 13 images, where we sbhojld be norht of 60.

The deploy was left for a sustained period to see if the issue resolved itself, and after 45-60 minutes, sensor was purged again and deployed to see if it would get any further while checking "crictl images".  We got to 13 images, and the no further

[BroLin-28.4.6@gmfinancial 22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e log]$ sudo crictl images | wc -l
13

At this point it was suspected that there may have been an issue with the update images that had been copied/downalded to the sensor as part of the upgrade, possibly a network quality issue with the link, the customer stated the links are due to be upgraded at some point in the near future.

With the sensor stopped and purged, fresh images were downloaded using "sudo corelightctl sensor images download ; sudo corelightctl sensor images load -v", the sensor was then deployed using broala apply config and this time all of the images were populated (77'ish) and the sensor came up clean.

Post upgrade: "sudo corelightctl sensor download" puts the images into /opt/corelight, this affects the filesystem usage in the root fiilesystem, and it doesn't look like there is a way to housekeep these images as they may be required for the running of the sensor. This may be a bug where they should go into /data possibly, will need to investigte further.

Summary, customer upgraded several sensors to 28.4.6, most came back online but 22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e was reporting driver issues fro napatech drivers.

Displaying active NIC firmware …
FPGA BANK0 active 9500 28.07 4x1G dedup: True shunt: False fec: False
FPGA BANK1             9500 28.07 4x1G dedup: True shunt: False fec: False

Available images:
9500 28.11 4x1G dedup: True shunt: False fec: False <<<<<<
 
New version burned to bank1 and toggled, sensor power cycled to come up clean, the sensor was then purged, deployed (broala-apply-config), and checking the pods it looked like it had hung on the coredns, the pods were stuck in initalising, "crictl images" showed 13 images, where we sbhojld be norht of 60. 
 
The deploy was left for a sustained period to see if the issue resolved itself, and after 45-60 minutes, sensor was purged again and deployed to see if it would get any further while checking "crictl images".  We got to 13 images, and the no further
 
[BroLin-28.4.6@gmfinancial 22-6c-39-c6-e0-68-40-8e log]$ sudo crictl images | wc -l
13
 
At this point it was suspected that there may have been an issue with the update images that had been copied/downalded to the sensor as part of the upgrade, possibly a network quality issue with the link, the customer stated the links are due to be upgraded at some point in the near future.
 
With the sensor stopped and purged, fresh images were downloaded using "sudo corelightctl sensor images download ; sudo corelightctl sensor images load -v", the sensor was then deployed using broala apply config and this time all of the images were populated (77'ish) and the sensor came up clean.
 
Post upgrade: "sudo corelightctl sensor download" puts the images into /opt/corelight, this affects the filesystem usage in the root fiilesystem, and it doesn't look like there is a way to housekeep these images as they may be required for the running of the sensor. This may be a bug where they should go into /data possibly, will need to investigte further.

article.title: Configure the 10G Management NIC
What SFP modules are supported on the monitoring NIC of the Corelight sensor ?
Support Agents - Disk Failure notice and RMA

events.body:
Based on your description, if the monitoring NIC shows no LED activity and the SFP works in other hardware, this suggests a hardware issue with the NIC or its connection to the sensor. For Corelight appliances, if the NIC is not recognized or the LEDs do not light up, you should check that the NIC is properly seated, the correct SFP is used, and the firmware is up to date. If the problem persists after reseating and firmware checks, an RMA (hardware replacement) is appropriate, as discussed in your correspondence. For AP 1001/3000, follow the NIC installation and troubleshooting guide and contact Corelight Support if the issue remains unresolved.

If you cannot enable remote access, physical troubleshooting (reseating NIC, checking cables, and SFP compatibility) is required. If the sensor is under warranty and the NIC remains non-functional after these steps, proceed with the RMA as planned. For more details on supported SFPs and troubleshooting steps, see this article and installation instructions.

Would you like a step-by-step breakdown of the physical troubleshooting and RMA process?

Sources:
- this article

Hi Halim,

Thank you for confirming that the replacement monitoring NIC has now arrived.

As discussed, you can find the instructions for replacing the Monitoring NIC in the sensor here:
https://docs.corelight.cloud/docs/sensor/hardware-guides/monitoring-nic-install.html#installation-instructions

**Please note:** You will encounter a warranty void if removed sticker during the replacement process. Because you are performing this replacement under the guidance of Corelight Support, breaking this seal will **NOT** void this sensor's warranty.

Please let me know if you run into any issues or have any questions during the replacement.

Thanks,
Travis Anderson

Thank you for choosing UPS. Please review the following instructions regarding the documentation that must accompany your shipment.
Provide the Correct Documentation
• Government regulations require documents that provide specific information about your international shipment.
• Documentation regulations and requirements for individual commodities vary from country to country. It is essential to review the commodity-specific
documentation necessary for both the country of origin and the country of destination. Depending on the commodity and its use, accompanying
documents may require special notations and licenses.
• The shipper also referred to as exporter of record, is responsible for providing complete and accurate information for export manifesting and customs
clearance in the destination country. It may be necessary to contact the company or person who arranged for the shipment of goods in order to verify
the information and forms that must be submitted with the shipment.
• High declared value shipments must be accompanied by UPS documentation that lists the package and instructions for preparation. Please follow
these instructions and present the completed documentation to a UPS driver or UPS customer service representative.
Providing a Complete Invoice
• The invoice information is used for customs clearance and the assessment of any applicable duties or taxes charged by the destination country.
• Be sure to include the original invoice and two copies of the invoice with the shipment for proper processing.
• Review your documents and verify the accuracy of all fields.
To ensure your shipment is processed properly, you must sign the invoice.
International Shipping Assistance
• Visit ups.com:
     · To download a commercial invoice or other international forms
     · To find a drop-off location near you
     · To arrange a pickup

• For assistance from the company you are shipping to, contact them directly.

 Join Microsoft Teams Meeting<https://www.google.com/url?q=https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%253ameeting_YWIyNmQ1NWEtNGMxYi00NzUyLWJhNmQtZTA1OWI0YmI3ZGFh%2540thread.v2/0?context%3D%257b%2522Tid%2522%253a%2522e140113a-9d75-4cb3-995d-dd213e0647d7%2522%252c%2522Oid%2522%253a%25224c9a1559-9487-4bc4-ac89-64724e67bff7%2522%257d%26launchAgent%3DGSuiteAddOn%26correlationId%3D837e10a3-1771-4e42-b273-dcf85c413f51&sa=D&source=calendar&usg=AOvVaw0axLh8H8VMUIq0wo7IUOmk>

ID: 245769882499945

Passcode: sL2m5vg3 

 Thanks,

Travis 

 Travis Anderson | Lead Technical Account Manager 

 p: (61) 419 410 424

travis.anderson@corelight.com<mailto:travis.anderson@corelight.com> 

 From: ABD HALIM MAHMOOD <halim@eclogic.io>

Date: Thursday, 7 May 2026 at 2:11 pm

To: Travis Anderson <travis.anderson@corelight.com>; Farhan Khan <farhan.khan@corelight.com>

Cc: Mohd Akbar Abu Bakar <akbar@eclogic.io>; AMIR HAMZAH HURI <amir@eclogic.io>; Syakir Ameer Roslan <syakir@eclogic.io>

Subject: Monitoring NIC Problem 

 Hi team, 

 We having an issue on sensor UID 84-88-65-9f-41-ad-6a-e0

There are no network traffic detected,

Client already check the sensor: 

  1.

There are no LED blinking at the NIC

 2.

They already test the SFP to other hardware and it working. 

 We cannot turn on remote service to this sensor.

Can we set a meeting and let me diagnose the issue. 

 Kind regards,

Halim

comments.body:
Based on your description, if the monitoring NIC shows no LED activity and the SFP works in other hardware, this suggests a hardware issue with the NIC or its connection to the sensor. For Corelight appliances, if the NIC is not recognized or the LEDs do not light up, you should check that the NIC is properly seated, the correct SFP is used, and the firmware is up to date. If the problem persists after reseating and firmware checks, an RMA (hardware replacement) is appropriate, as discussed in your correspondence. For AP 1001/3000, follow the NIC installation and troubleshooting guide and contact Corelight Support if the issue remains unresolved.

If you cannot enable remote access, physical troubleshooting (reseating NIC, checking cables, and SFP compatibility) is required. If the sensor is under warranty and the NIC remains non-functional after these steps, proceed with the RMA as planned. For more details on supported SFPs and troubleshooting steps, see this article and installation instructions.

Would you like a step-by-step breakdown of the physical troubleshooting and RMA process?

Sources:
- this article

The sensor is air-gapped, designated for Customer MINDEF Malaysia, with partner ECLogic. During a troubleshooting call, Observed an "IPC connection error" on the monitoring NIC. After troubleshooting, it was decided to proceed with the RMA process to replace the NIC.

Troubleshooting followed from this guru card: https://app.getguru.com/card/cay9M8gi/Error-NtService-has-failed-and-IPC-Connection-Error-0x10040005

Hi Halim,

I appreciate your update on this case, and it is great to hear the replacement was successful.

Could you please organise the return of the faulty card using the attached return label when you have the opportunity? This will allow us to close this case and mark it as resolved.

The instructions are on the first page of the attached pdf. If you have any questions, feel free to reach out to us.

Thanks,
Travis Anderson

Thank you for choosing UPS. Please review the following instructions regarding the documentation that must accompany your shipment.
Provide the Correct Documentation
• Government regulations require documents that provide specific information about your international shipment.
• Documentation regulations and requirements for individual commodities vary from country to country. It is essential to review the commodity-specific
documentation necessary for both the country of origin and the country of destination. Depending on the commodity and its use, accompanying
documents may require special notations and licenses.
• The shipper also referred to as exporter of record, is responsible for providing complete and accurate information for export manifesting and customs
clearance in the destination country. It may be necessary to contact the company or person who arranged for the shipment of goods in order to verify
the information and forms that must be submitted with the shipment.
• High declared value shipments must be accompanied by UPS documentation that lists the package and instructions for preparation. Please follow
these instructions and present the completed documentation to a UPS driver or UPS customer service representative.
Providing a Complete Invoice
• The invoice information is used for customs clearance and the assessment of any applicable duties or taxes charged by the destination country.
• Be sure to include the original invoice and two copies of the invoice with the shipment for proper processing.
• Review your documents and verify the accuracy of all fields.
To ensure your shipment is processed properly, you must sign the invoice.
International Shipping Assistance
• Visit ups.com:
     · To download a commercial invoice or other international forms
     · To find a drop-off location near you
     · To arrange a pickup
     · For additional information on how to contact UPS
• For assistance from the company you are shipping to, contact them directly.

Provide the Correct Documentation

• Government regulations require documents that provide specific information about your international shipment.

• Documentation regulations and requirements for individual commodities vary from country to country. It is essential to review the commodity-specific

documentation necessary for both the country of origin and the country of destination. Depending on the commodity and its use, accompanying

documents may require special notations and licenses.

• The shipper also referred to as exporter of record, is responsible for providing complete and accurate information for export manifesting and customs

clearance in the destination country. It may be necessary to contact the company or person who arranged for the shipment of goods in order to verify

the information and forms that must be submitted with the shipment.

• High declared value shipments must be accompanied by UPS documentation that lists the package and instructions for preparation. Please follow

these instructions and present the completed documentation to a UPS driver or UPS customer service representative.

Providing a Complete Invoice

• The invoice information is used for customs clearance and the assessment of any applicable duties or taxes charged by the destination country.

• Be sure to include the original invoice and two copies of the invoice with the shipment for proper processing.

• Review your documents and verify the accuracy of all fields.

To ensure your shipment is processed properly, you must sign the invoice.

International Shipping Assistance

tickets.subject: Sensor RAID fault
tickets.description:
Hello!

We are once again experiencing RAID faults on one of our sensors, this time the Sensor UID is: f0-c6-c3-85-66-92-f0-4e. We would like to have a replacement sensor sent ASAP. Seeing as the AP200 platform is no longer for sale, we'd argue for a AP220. Rebuilding the RAID that you tend to propose has solved the issue 0 times, it only prolongs the inevitable.

It's getting to the point internally where most of our operative time is spent returning and re-installing Corelights at our customers and it's becoming increasingly frustrating to defend its use. 

Additionally, we recently purchased a "new" sensor (Serial: 103-000000000095) from you, and when unboxing it this morning to install as a replacement we see that the entire server enclosure (metal) is completely scratched (on both sides), the "ears" are bent and you have sent the wrong rack-mounting rails AGAIN. It genuinely looks like the hardware has been in use and seen 5 difficult years. When booting it, it completely refuses to boot, we assume it is broken out of the box.....

Please, help us resolve this and finding a way forward. What i'd like initially is:
1) TWO replacement sensors sent ASAP. As AP200 is no longer for sale and the platform has been INCREDIBLY unstable, the AP220 seems appropriate.
2) A set of replacement rails for the recently purchased AP200 sent alongside it's replacement.

Moving forward, i'd like to have a talk about how we can work together to ensure our customers receive quality hardware.

Kind regards
Filip

events.body:
It seems likely that for AP 200 sensors, Corelight’s policy is to replace the entire unit for RMAs, as none of the hardware components are hot-swappable. Since the AP 200 is end-of-sale, you can request a replacement, but Corelight will continue to support AP 200 with valid subscriptions until April 2031. However, there is no explicit guarantee in the data sources that an AP 220 will be provided as a replacement for an AP 200; hardware upgrades or trade-ins are not currently offered as standard policy. For damaged or non-functional new hardware, you should contact Corelight Support directly for RMA processing and to request correct rails and a working replacement unit. Please note that all returned hardware is wiped of customer data upon arrival at Corelight facilities, and you can follow the documented process to clear data before shipping if desired.

For urgent RMA requests and to discuss ongoing hardware quality concerns, you should open a support case via the Corelight Support Portal, email support@corelight.com, or call the support line. For escalation or to discuss broader partnership and quality issues, it is recommended to request a meeting with your Corelight account team or TAM. If you need to save your configuration for a replacement, follow the RMA configuration backup/restore process. For more detail or step-by-step guidance, please let me know.

Sources:
- What is the Corelight Support and End of Sale (EOS) Policy
- RMA configuration backup/restore process

events.body:
Hi Filip,

After sharing your photos with our Hardware Team, and discussing the issue in depth, I have the following information for you.

Regarding Sensor 103-000000000095, this was an RMA (refurbished unit) that was shipped to replace the faulty Sensor ending in 881 (RMA 84302) that was raised last year and therefore not a new Sensor as you believed it to be.

As you are encountering an issue with this Sensor, I will raise a new ticket for this Sensor and have an RMA raised for it. Do you happen to have the original box that this Sensor arrived in, as this might provide details as to why the ears are bent?

Regarding Sensor f0-c6-c3-85-66-92-f0-4e, this was shipped to you in 2022, and I believe this is the first issue you have raised after it has been in use for 3 1/2 years.

Are you able to share with us the licencing utilisation from your Fleet Manager UI regarding this Sensor, as we would be interested to see the kind of throughput it has been experiencing for the last year?

Kind regards,

Michael Munt

Regarding Sensor 103-000000000095, this was an RMA (refurbished unit) that was shipped to replace the faulty Sensor ending in 881 (RMA 84302) that was raised last year and therefore not a new Sensor as you believed it to be. 
 
As you are encountering an issue with this Sensor, I will raise a new ticket for this Sensor and have an RMA raised for it. Do you happen to have the original box that this Sensor arrived in, as this might provide details as to why the ears are bent?

comments.body:
Hello!

We are once again experiencing RAID faults on one of our sensors, this time the Sensor UID is: f0-c6-c3-85-66-92-f0-4e. We would like to have a replacement sensor sent ASAP. Seeing as the AP200 platform is no longer for sale, we'd argue for a AP220. Rebuilding the RAID that you tend to propose has solved the issue 0 times, it only prolongs the inevitable.

It's getting to the point internally where most of our operative time is spent returning and re-installing Corelights at our customers and it's becoming increasingly frustrating to defend its use. 

Additionally, we recently purchased a "new" sensor (Serial: 103-000000000095) from you, and when unboxing it this morning to install as a replacement we see that the entire server enclosure (metal) is completely scratched (on both sides), the "ears" are bent and you have sent the wrong rack-mounting rails AGAIN. It genuinely looks like the hardware has been in use and seen 5 difficult years. When booting it, it completely refuses to boot, we assume it is broken out of the box.....

Please, help us resolve this and finding a way forward. What i'd like initially is:
1) TWO replacement sensors sent ASAP. As AP200 is no longer for sale and the platform has been INCREDIBLY unstable, the AP220 seems appropriate.
2) A set of replacement rails for the recently purchased AP200 sent alongside it's replacement.

Moving forward, i'd like to have a talk about how we can work together to ensure our customers receive quality hardware.

Kind regards
Filip

comments.body:
It seems likely that for AP 200 sensors, Corelight’s policy is to replace the entire unit for RMAs, as none of the hardware components are hot-swappable. Since the AP 200 is end-of-sale, you can request a replacement, but Corelight will continue to support AP 200 with valid subscriptions until April 2031. However, there is no explicit guarantee in the data sources that an AP 220 will be provided as a replacement for an AP 200; hardware upgrades or trade-ins are not currently offered as standard policy. For damaged or non-functional new hardware, you should contact Corelight Support directly for RMA processing and to request correct rails and a working replacement unit. Please note that all returned hardware is wiped of customer data upon arrival at Corelight facilities, and you can follow the documented process to clear data before shipping if desired.

For urgent RMA requests and to discuss ongoing hardware quality concerns, you should open a support case via the Corelight Support Portal, email support@corelight.com, or call the support line. For escalation or to discuss broader partnership and quality issues, it is recommended to request a meeting with your Corelight account team or TAM. If you need to save your configuration for a replacement, follow the RMA configuration backup/restore process. For more detail or step-by-step guidance, please let me know.

Sources:
- What is the Corelight Support and End of Sale (EOS) Policy
- RMA configuration backup/restore process

Hi Filip,

After sharing your photos with our Hardware Team, and discussing the issue in depth, I have the following information for you.

Regarding Sensor 103-000000000095, this was an RMA (refurbished unit) that was shipped to replace the faulty Sensor ending in 881 (RMA 84302) that was raised last year and therefore not a new Sensor as you believed it to be.

As you are encountering an issue with this Sensor, I will raise a new ticket for this Sensor and have an RMA raised for it. Do you happen to have the original box that this Sensor arrived in, as this might provide details as to why the ears are bent?

Regarding Sensor f0-c6-c3-85-66-92-f0-4e, this was shipped to you in 2022, and I believe this is the first issue you have raised after it has been in use for 3 1/2 years.

Are you able to share with us the licencing utilisation from your Fleet Manager UI regarding this Sensor, as we would be interested to see the kind of throughput it has been experiencing for the last year?

Kind regards,

Michael Munt

events.subject: [Zendesk] #{{ticket.id}} {{ current_user.name }} [{{ticket.organization.name}}]: {{ticket.title}}

events.body:
Common causes for sensors dropping traffic and stopping log export include monitoring ports not receiving traffic, mismatched link speeds, or service failures. Check the port status of the monitoring interfaces, confirm link speed matches the switch, and review service status for failures. Also, verify the configuration for log export and ensure the server-side settings match your sensor configuration. If all conn log history fields are uppercase or lowercase, the sensor may only see one direction of traffic, indicating a cabling or packet broker issue. Duplicate notices can also result from the sensor seeing the same traffic multiple times due to improper tap or packet broker configuration. For more, see Corelight troubleshooting common issues.

If the issue persists, check for known bugs in your sensor version, such as memory issues, exporter problems, or service failures, and consider rebooting the sensor or clearing data buffers. If the problem continues after these checks, contact Corelight Support for further diagnosis and remote troubleshooting. See Sensor Known Issues and Clearing Data Buffers.

Would you like a step-by-step troubleshooting guide?

Sources:
- Sensor Known Issues
- Clearing Data Buffers

If all conn log history fields are uppercase or lowercase, the sensor may only see one direction of traffic, indicating a cabling or packet broker issue. Duplicate notices can also result from the sensor seeing the same traffic multiple times due to improper tap or packet broker configuration. For more, see Corelight troubleshooting common issues.

If the issue persists, check for known bugs in your sensor version, such as memory issues, exporter problems, or service failures, and consider rebooting the sensor or clearing data buffers. comments.body:
Common causes for sensors dropping traffic and stopping log export include monitoring ports not receiving traffic, mismatched link speeds, or service failures. Check the port status of the monitoring interfaces, confirm link speed matches the switch, and review service status for failures. Also, verify the configuration for log export and ensure the server-side settings match your sensor configuration. If all conn log history fields are uppercase or lowercase, the sensor may only see one direction of traffic, indicating a cabling or packet broker issue. Duplicate notices can also result from the sensor seeing the same traffic multiple times due to improper tap or packet broker configuration. For more, see Corelight troubleshooting common issues.

If the issue persists, check for known bugs in your sensor version, such as memory issues, exporter problems, or service failures, and consider rebooting the sensor or clearing data buffers. If the problem continues after these checks, contact Corelight Support for further diagnosis and remote troubleshooting. See Sensor Known Issues and Clearing Data Buffers.

Would you like a step-by-step troubleshooting guide?

Sources:
- Sensor Known Issues
- Clearing Data Buffers

Hi Javed and Cameron,
We’re following up on the NGS Status error related to disk space for your Corelight Sensor UID `51-44-4e-09-74-85-ac-e4`.


    51-44-4e-09-74-85-ac-e4AP 5002108-000000000195release 28.4.02026-03-18 16:38:15.271 platform NGS Status error: system does not have adequate free disk space: 86.81GB disk space required, but only 84.84 free2026-03-18 16:37:41.745 platform Machine Learning Failed - Error Message:Unable to start service - Container waiting: corelight-ml


When you have a moment, could you please enable the remote interface on this sensor so we can proceed with further investigation? If you run into any issues enabling it or have any questions, let us know and we’ll be glad to help.


Best regards,

Edward Dominguez | Lead Escalation Engineer

events.body:
Summary:
1. The sensor is showing an NGS status error: "system does not have adequate free disk space" - requires 86.81GB but only 84.37GB free.

    /data partition usage 98% << 3.2TB of 3.5TBKafka logs (/data/kafka-ng) << 2.3TB (72% of /data)kafka.log.retention.bytes << 90GB per topic

2. The Kafka logs appear to be the culprit. Multiple topics are hitting ~85-88GB each (near the 90GB retention limit), and with 50+ topics, this accumulates to 2.3TB. The retention policy isn't keeping pace with data ingestion.

    BroLin-28.4.0@united-airlines 51-44-4e-09-74-85-ac-e4 kafka-logs]$ du -hsc * | grep -E "^[.0-9]+[G]" | sort -rn | head88G conn-087G dns-086G ssl-085G weird-085G suricata_eve-085G suricata_corelight-085G ldap_search-085G kerberos-085G http-085G genisys-085G files-085G etc_viz-085G dce_rpc-085G corelight_ad_ew_servers_services-085G bacnet_property-084G smb_files-084G ntp-084G ldap-0/>

events.body:
Escalated to Staff Eng via #support-only channel:


* * *
Edward Dominguez  [3:19 PM]
@Jed

 ![:dumpsterfire:](https://emoji.slack-edge.com/T031FBGH8/dumpsterfire/79bb3e833a10a058.gif) P2
Customer: united-airlines
ZD#: 29576
Description of issue: Sensor is reporting NGS status error: system does not have adequate free disk space: 86.81GB disk space required, but only 84.37 free
Sensor UID: 51-44-4e-09-74-85-ac-e4
Release version: 28.4.0
Remote access possible Y/N: Y
If yes, what changes permitted: Observe Only
Online/Offline: Online
Metrics link (if online): https://humio.corelight.io/corelight_cloud_logs/dashboards/Examine%20a%20System?%24uid=%5B%2251-44-4e-09-74-85-ac-e4%22%5D&dashboardId=9jYnTIuAMcfUsda9OOpulEhF9ZIeErZ3&fullscreen=false&sharedTime=true&start=24h&tz=Etc%2FUTC&updateFrequency=realtime

 ![:test_tube:](https://a.slack-edge.com/production-standard-emoji-assets/15.0/apple-medium/1f9ea.png) Observations

- `/data` partition is at 98% utilization (3.2TB of 3.5TB)
- Kafka logs (`/data/kafka-ng`) are consuming approximately 2.3TB
- `kafka.log.retention.bytes` is configured at 90GB per topic
- Multiple Kafka topics appear to be at or near the 90GB retention limit (ranging from 85-88GB each)
- The cumulative size of Kafka logs suggests retention may not be enforced as expected


Logs / evidence:

    df -h /data:/dev/mapper/data 3.5T 3.2T 73G 98% /data

Top disk consumers under /data:

    2.3T ./kafka-ng869G ./sensor-core38G ./prometheus-ng

    BroLin-28.4.0@united-airlines 51-44-4e-09-74-85-ac-e4 kafka-logs]$ du -hsc * | grep -E "^[.0-9]+[G]" | sort -rn | head88G conn-087G dns-086G ssl-085G weird-085G suricata_eve-085G suricata_corelight-085G ldap_search-085G kerberos-085G http-085G genisys-0~/


Kafka retention setting:

    kafka.log.retention.bytes = 90000000000 (90GB)


 **![:point_right:](https://a.slack-edge.com/production-standard-emoji-assets/15.0/apple-medium/1f449.png) Requested Input:**
This issue appears to be similar to ZD#29410 (similar disk space exhaustion scenario).
I  suspect this may be a regression of PHOTON-4623, where Kafka log retention was not properly managing disk space.

1. Could there be a new condition causing similar behavior that was not included in the patch from PHOTON-4623?
2. Please advise on recommended remediation steps or suggest this issue should be escalated to #eng-escalation channel.

events.body:
Jed Daniels (he/him)  1 minute ago

`/data/sensor-core/ml/anomaly-detection` is 868G, but I don't think that is actually the problem. I think the problem is that some processes aren't releasing deleted files properly. This is evidenced by the discrepancy between the Total (3.5T), Used (3.2T) and available (81G, currently), and by the ~1800 matches when you run `lsof | grep '(deleted)'` . The immediate tactical fix is to have them reboot the sensor. This will restart all processes and free up the "deleted" space that hasn't yet been reclaimed. If they are unable to reboot, let me know and we can discuss alternatives.
events.html_body:
Jed Daniels (he/him)  1 minute ago
 
/data/sensor-core/ml/anomaly-detection is 868G, but I don't think that is actually the problem. I think the problem is that some processes aren't releasing deleted files properly. This is evidenced by the discrepancy between the Total (3.5T), Used (3.2T) and available (81G, currently), and by the ~1800 matches when you run lsof | grep '(deleted)' . The immediate tactical fix is to have them reboot the sensor. This will restart all processes and free up the "deleted" space that hasn't yet been reclaimed. If they are unable to reboot, let me know and we can discuss alternatives.

events.body:
You are registered as a CC on this support request ({{ticket.id}}). Reply to this email to add a comment to the request.

{{ticket.comments_formatted}}

Hey Edward,

I did reboot the device a couple of times now with no progress. I also tried flushing the logs to make space, but the sensor won’t allow it. It also does not seem to be taking any config updates from the Fleet Manger. I disconnected it from Fleet and tried to apply changed directly on the box, but it would not accept them there either. This is a real problem at this point as half of the traffic at that site is going to this sensor. In the past I have been able to disable FEC on the monitoring NIC to functionally disable it and trick the Gigamon infra to send all of the traffic to the other sensor, but because the config updates are failing, I am unable to do that in this case. I would like to escalate this to a P1 at this time as we are losing ~50% of the traffic.

Cheers,

Cameron

Hey Edward,

I did reboot the device a couple of times now with no progress. I also tried flushing the logs to make space, but the sensor won’t allow it. It also does not seem to be taking any config updates from the Fleet Manger. I disconnected it from Fleet and tried to apply changed directly on the box, but it would not accept them there either. This is a real problem at this point as half of the traffic at that site is going to this sensor. In the past I have been able to disable FEC on the monitoring NIC to functionally disable it and trick the Gigamon infra to send all of the traffic to the other sensor, but because the config updates are failing, I am unable to do that in this case. I would like to escalate this to a P1 at this time as we are losing ~50% of the traffic.

Cheers,

Cameron

events.plain_body:
Hey Edward, 

 I did reboot the device a couple of times now with no progress. I also tried flushing the logs to make space, but the sensor won’t allow it. It also does not seem to be taking any config updates from the Fleet Manger. I disconnected it from Fleet and tried to apply changed directly on the box, but it would not accept them there either. This is a real problem at this point as half of the traffic at that site is going to this sensor. In the past I have been able to disable FEC on the monitoring NIC to functionally disable it and trick the Gigamon infra to send all of the traffic to the other sensor, but because the config updates are failing, I am unable to do that in this case. I would like to escalate this to a P1 at this time as we are losing ~50% of the traffic. 

 Cheers, 

 Cameron

comments.body:
Summary:
1. The sensor is showing an NGS status error: "system does not have adequate free disk space" - requires 86.81GB but only 84.37GB free.

    /data partition usage 98% << 3.2TB of 3.5TBKafka logs (/data/kafka-ng) << 2.3TB (72% of /data)kafka.log.retention.bytes << 90GB per topic

2. The Kafka logs appear to be the culprit. Multiple topics are hitting ~85-88GB each (near the 90GB retention limit), and with 50+ topics, this accumulates to 2.3TB. The retention policy isn't keeping pace with data ingestion.

    BroLin-28.4.0@united-airlines 51-44-4e-09-74-85-ac-e4 kafka-logs]$ du -hsc * | grep -E "^[.0-9]+[G]" | sort -rn | head88G conn-087G dns-086G ssl-085G weird-085G suricata_eve-085G suricata_corelight-085G ldap_search-085G kerberos-085G http-085G genisys-085G files-085G etc_viz-085G dce_rpc-085G corelight_ad_ew_servers_services-085G bacnet_property-084G smb_files-084G ntp-084G ldap-0/>

Escalated to Staff Eng via #support-only channel:


* * *
Edward Dominguez  [3:19 PM]
@Jed

 ![:dumpsterfire:](https://emoji.slack-edge.com/T031FBGH8/dumpsterfire/79bb3e833a10a058.gif) P2
Customer: united-airlines
ZD#: 29576
Description of issue: Sensor is reporting NGS status error: system does not have adequate free disk space: 86.81GB disk space required, but only 84.37 free
Sensor UID: 51-44-4e-09-74-85-ac-e4
Release version: 28.4.0
Remote access possible Y/N: Y
If yes, what changes permitted: Observe Only
Online/Offline: Online
Metrics link (if online): https://humio.corelight.io/corelight_cloud_logs/dashboards/Examine%20a%20System?%24uid=%5B%2251-44-4e-09-74-85-ac-e4%22%5D&dashboardId=9jYnTIuAMcfUsda9OOpulEhF9ZIeErZ3&fullscreen=false&sharedTime=true&start=24h&tz=Etc%2FUTC&updateFrequency=realtime

 ![:test_tube:](https://a.slack-edge.com/production-standard-emoji-assets/15.0/apple-medium/1f9ea.png) Observations

- `/data` partition is at 98% utilization (3.2TB of 3.5TB)
- Kafka logs (`/data/kafka-ng`) are consuming approximately 2.3TB
- `kafka.log.retention.bytes` is configured at 90GB per topic
- Multiple Kafka topics appear to be at or near the 90GB retention limit (ranging from 85-88GB each)
- The cumulative size of Kafka logs suggests retention may not be enforced as expected


Logs / evidence:

    df -h /data:/dev/mapper/data 3.5T 3.2T 73G 98% /data

Top disk consumers under /data:

    2.3T ./kafka-ng869G ./sensor-core38G ./prometheus-ng

    BroLin-28.4.0@united-airlines 51-44-4e-09-74-85-ac-e4 kafka-logs]$ du -hsc * | grep -E "^[.0-9]+[G]" | sort -rn | head88G conn-087G dns-086G ssl-085G weird-085G suricata_eve-085G suricata_corelight-085G ldap_search-085G kerberos-085G http-085G genisys-0~/


Kafka retention setting:

    kafka.log.retention.bytes = 90000000000 (90GB)


 **![:point_right:](https://a.slack-edge.com/production-standard-emoji-assets/15.0/apple-medium/1f449.png) Requested Input:**
This issue appears to be similar to ZD#29410 (similar disk space exhaustion scenario).
I  suspect this may be a regression of PHOTON-4623, where Kafka log retention was not properly managing disk space.

1. Could there be a new condition causing similar behavior that was not included in the patch from PHOTON-4623?
2. Please advise on recommended remediation steps or suggest this issue should be escalated to #eng-escalation channel.

comments.body:
Jed Daniels (he/him)  1 minute ago

`/data/sensor-core/ml/anomaly-detection` is 868G, but I don't think that is actually the problem. I think the problem is that some processes aren't releasing deleted files properly. This is evidenced by the discrepancy between the Total (3.5T), Used (3.2T) and available (81G, currently), and by the ~1800 matches when you run `lsof | grep '(deleted)'` . The immediate tactical fix is to have them reboot the sensor. This will restart all processes and free up the "deleted" space that hasn't yet been reclaimed. If they are unable to reboot, let me know and we can discuss alternatives.
comments.html_body:

/data/sensor-core/ml/anomaly-detection is 868G, but I don't think that is actually the problem. I think the problem is that some processes aren't releasing deleted files properly. This is evidenced by the discrepancy between the Total (3.5T), Used (3.2T) and available (81G, currently), and by the ~1800 matches when you run lsof | grep '(deleted)' . The immediate tactical fix is to have them reboot the sensor. This will restart all processes and free up the "deleted" space that hasn't yet been reclaimed. If they are unable to reboot, let me know and we can discuss alternatives.

comments.body:
Hey Edward,

I did reboot the device a couple of times now with no progress. I also tried flushing the logs to make space, but the sensor won’t allow it. It also does not seem to be taking any config updates from the Fleet Manger. I disconnected it from Fleet and tried to apply changed directly on the box, but it would not accept them there either. This is a real problem at this point as half of the traffic at that site is going to this sensor. In the past I have been able to disable FEC on the monitoring NIC to functionally disable it and trick the Gigamon infra to send all of the traffic to the other sensor, but because the config updates are failing, I am unable to do that in this case. I would like to escalate this to a P1 at this time as we are losing ~50% of the traffic.

Cheers,

Cameron

Hey Edward, 

 I did reboot the device a couple of times now with no progress. I also tried flushing the logs to make space, but the sensor won’t allow it. It also does not seem to be taking any config updates from the Fleet Manger. I disconnected it from Fleet and tried to apply changed directly on the box, but it would not accept them there either. This is a real problem at this point as half of the traffic at that site is going to this sensor. In the past I have been able to disable FEC on the monitoring NIC to functionally disable it and trick the Gigamon infra to send all of the traffic to the other sensor, but because the config updates are failing, I am unable to do that in this case. I would like to escalate this to a P1 at this time as we are losing ~50% of the traffic. 

 Cheers, 

 Cameron
